data.title: Market Chatter: Meta Cuts Employee Stock Awards Amid AI Spending
data.description:
Meta Platforms is reducing equity-based compensation by about 10% for most employees this year while continuing to invest in artificial intelligence projects, The Financial Times reported Thursday, citing sources familiar with the matter.

Meta employees typically receive equity refreshers on top of their base salaries and annual bonuses, according to the report. This year's adjustments may vary based on location and job level, FT added.

Meta did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' request for comment.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: First Majestic Silver Price Target Maintained With a $11.00/Share by HC Wainwright & Co.
BRADY CORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Brady Corp, a global leader in identification solutions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the first quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial and operational performance, highlighting key metrics and strategic initiatives that have shaped its recent results.

Financial Highlights

Brady Corp reported robust financial performance for the first quarter of 2025, driven by organic sales growth and strategic acquisitions. Key financial metrics include:

  • Net Sales: $356.7 million, an increase of 10.6% compared to the same period in the prior year.
  • Gross Margin: $175.8 million, representing 49.3% of net sales, down from 50.2% in the prior year due to facility closure and reorganization costs.
  • Operating Income: $51.2 million, a decrease of 5.0% from the prior year, impacted by non-recurring acquisition-related costs and facility closure expenses.
  • Net Income: $40.3 million, down from $43.6 million in the prior year, reflecting increased interest expense and acquisition-related costs.
  • Net Income per Class A Nonvoting Common Share - Basic: $0.84, decreased from $0.90 in the prior year.
  • Net Income per Class A Nonvoting Common Share - Diluted: $0.83, decreased from $0.90 in the prior year.
  • Net Income per Class B Voting Common Share - Basic: $0.84, decreased from $0.90 in the prior year.
  • Net Income per Class B Voting Common Share - Diluted: $0.83, decreased from $0.90 in the prior year.

Business Highlights

Brady Corp's operational performance in the first quarter of 2025 was marked by significant growth in key segments and regions, alongside strategic initiatives aimed at long-term growth. Key business highlights include:

  • Revenue Segments: The Americas & Asia segment experienced organic sales growth of 4.3% in the three months ended January 31, 2025, driven by increases in wire identification, safety and facility identification, and product identification product lines. The Europe & Australia segment saw a 15.1% sales growth from acquisitions, although organic sales declined by 0.8%.
  • Geographical Performance: In the Americas, organic sales increased in the low-single digits for the three months ended January 31, 2025, with mid-single-digit growth for the six months. Asia saw an 11% organic sales increase in the three months ended January 31, 2025, primarily driven by growth in Japan and India, offset by a decline in China.
  • New Product Launches: The company continues to invest in new product development, particularly in printing systems and materials, to drive long-term organic sales growth.
  • Future Outlook: Brady Corp plans to continue integrating recent acquisitions to enhance its strategic position and accelerate long-term sales growth. The company also aims to drive operational excellence and execute sustainable efficiency gains within its global operations.

SEC Filing: BRADY CORP [ BRC ] - 10-Q - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: Singapore Central Bank Announces Measures to Boost Equities Market
data.description:
By Amanda Lee

SINGAPORE--Singapore has announced a set of measures aimed at strengthening the city-state's equities market, including changes to draw more quality listings amid sluggish dealmaking activity.

The package features some tax incentives laid out in the 2025 budget, and a streamlining of public listing processes to make Singapore's equities market more competitive, the Monetary Authority of Singapore said Friday.

The measures were announced by a review group set up by the central bank last year to boost the local market's appeal to investors and companies seeking to list and access growth capital.

"Singapore's listing process will be significantly more efficient and streamlined than now and will compare favorably with leading financial hubs," the MAS said in a statement.

Part of that will involve consolidating review functions for listing suitability and prospectus disclosures to offer greater clarity on the IPO process and timelines, it said.

A 20% corporate income tax rebate will also be available for new primary listings and a 10% tax rebate for new secondary listings.

"These measures aim to attract companies with operations in Singapore and fund managers to tap Singapore's equities market for their capital raising," the central bank said.

On the demand side, the new measures aim to increase investor interest by deepening trading liquidity and strengthening capabilities in local fund management and equity research, the MAS said.

To that end, a S$5 billion program will be established via which the central bank will invest with select fund managers with the capacity to implement investment mandates that are strongly focused on Singapore stocks.

The review group also recommended adopting a more pro-enterprise regulatory stance, alongside other initiatives to bolster investor confidence, the statement showed.

"We hope that these measures would address the multifaceted challenges that Singapore's equities market faces today," the MAS said.

The central bank and the Singapore Exchange Regulation will issue detailed consultations on the proposals by mid-2025, and will consider further ones as well.

The initiatives announced will jumpstart more capital into the capital markets ecosystem and stimulate interest in local stocks and listings, said SGX Group CEO Loh Boon Chye.

Write to Amanda Lee at amanda.lee@wsj.com

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Friday at 7 AM ET

Private-equity executives entered 2025 with high hopes for a dealmaking recovery that is lifting payouts to fund investors and powering fundraising.

Trump's Energy Plans Are Creating Surprising Opportunities for Investors

It's not all about oil, one of the president's top priorities. Why natural gas leads our list of the industry's most promising investments. Even some solar and wind stocks could climb.

French Industry Sentiment Improves Despite Tariff Threat

U.K. Retail Sales Rise for First Time in Five Months

Retailers in the U.K. reported a brighter start to the new year, with sales rising after four straight months of falls, largely from a recovery in the food segment.

data.title: Atmus Filtration Technologies 4Q Sales Increase Lifts Profit
data.description:
By Denny Jacob

Atmus Filtration Technologies on Friday posted an increase in earnings as sales rose in its fourth quarter.

The Nashville, Tenn., filtration company logged net income of $40.1 million, or 48 cents a share, compared to $34.8 million, or 42 cents a share, in the prior-year period.

Stripping out certain one-time items, earnings came in at 58 cents a share. Analysts polled by FactSet expected 53 cents a share.

Sales rose to $406.7 million from $399.7 million. Analysts polled by FactSet expected $399.2 million.

Atmus attributed the increase in sales primarily to higher volume and prices.

Atmus forecast revenue between $1.67 billion and $1.74 billion as well as adjusted earnings per-share in the range of $2.35 and $2.60 for 2025, in line with analysts' estimates.

Shares rose 7% to $42 in premarket trading.

Write to Denny Jacob at denny.jacob@wsj.com

data.title: TXNM Energy Reports 2024 Earnings and Updates Growth Targets
data.description:
TXNM Energy, an energy holding company based in Albuquerque, New Mexico, has released its 8-K filing for the fiscal year 2024. The company, which delivers energy to over 800,000 homes and businesses across Texas and New Mexico, has reported its financial results and provided updates on its growth outlook.

Financial Highlights

For the year 2024, TXNM Energy reported GAAP earnings of $2.67 per diluted share, a significant increase from $1.02 per diluted share in 2023. Ongoing earnings were $2.74 per diluted share, aligning with the company's guidance expectations. The company introduced its 2025 ongoing earnings guidance range at $2.74 to $2.84 per diluted share, reflecting strong growth in Texas and the phased implementation of customer rate increases in New Mexico.

Business and Operational Highlights

TXNM Energy's 5-year capital investment plan has been updated to cover the years 2025-2029, with a total investment of $7.8 billion, a 26% increase over the previous plan. This includes additional investments in Texas transmission projects resulting from the ERCOT Permian Basin Reliability Study. The regulated rate base is expected to grow by 12% under this plan, with earnings per share targeted to grow between 7% and 9%.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

The company has announced a phased-in rate increase for PNM customers in New Mexico to help manage the impact on customers. Additionally, TXNM Energy has focused on expanding grid infrastructure to support growth and reliability in its Texas service territory.

Management's Perspective

Pat Vincent-Collawn, TXNM Energy Chairman and CEO, stated, "Our financial expectations are driven by the continued expansion of grid infrastructure supporting growth and reliability in our Texas service territory. In New Mexico, the proposed settlement in our rate review is phased-in to assist our customers in managing the impact. As we look forward, we will continue to prioritize investments for a more reliable, resilient grid that will provide direct benefits to our customers in New Mexico and Texas for years to come."

Future Outlook

TXNM Energy has provided a consolidated ongoing earnings guidance range of $2.74 to $2.84 per diluted share for 2025. The company aims to achieve long-term earnings per share growth of 7% to 9%, driven by its updated capital investment plan and strategic initiatives.

SEC Filing: TXNM ENERGY INC [ TXNM ] - 8-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: L3Harris Technologies Begins Construction on Arkansas Solid Rocket Motor Facility
data.description:
L3Harris Technologies said Thursday it has begun construction on four new solid rocket motor production facilities at its Camden, Arkansas, site.

The company said the expansion is part of a $215.6 million deal between a Department of Defense program to increase domestic rocket propulsion manufacturing capacity.

The new construction will include a 60,000-square-foot facility to centralize production of one program, reducing the distance the motors travel in manufacturing by 80%, the company said.

data.title: Balchem Q4 Adjusted Earnings, Net Sales Rise
data.description:
Balchem Corp. reported Q4 adjusted earnings Friday of $1.13 per diluted share, up from $0.95 a year earlier.

Two analysts polled by FactSet expected $1.11.

Net sales for the quarter ended Dec. 31 were $240 million, up from $228.7 million a year earlier.

Three analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $240 million.

data.title: Jefferies Downgrades Airbus to Hold, Trims PT
data.description: Jefferies on Friday downgraded French aerospace company Airbus to hold from buy and reduced its price target to 180 euros from 190 euros.

data.title: German Sovereign Credit Default Swaps Stay Steady But Elections Pose Risks — Market Talk
data.description: Germany's sovereign credit default swaps, which protect against the risk of the country defaulting, stay steady ahead of German elections this weekend. The elections pose risks for investors, says Karsten Junius at J. Safra Sarasin Sustainable Asset Management. Friedrich Merz of the Christian Democratic Union is likely to become the next chancellor. However, it is unclear whether traditional parties will secure at least two thirds of the seats in parliament. This would raise concerns over whether they could change the constitution and reform the debt brake rule without the support of populist parties, the asset manager's chief economist says. Germany's five-year CDS stays unchanged at 13 basis points, S&P Global Market Intelligence data show. (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

data.title: Core Scientific, Inc. Schedules Fourth Quarter Fiscal Year 2024 Earnings Release, Conference Call and Webcast
data.description:
Core Scientific, Inc. Schedules Fourth Quarter Fiscal Year 2024 Earnings Release, Conference Call and Webcast

AUSTIN, Texas--(BUSINESS WIRE)--February 21, 2025--

Core Scientific, Inc. ("Core Scientific" or "the Company"), a leader in digital infrastructure for high-performance computing and bitcoin mining, today announced it will issue financial results for the Company's fiscal year 2024 fourth quarter after financial markets close on Wednesday, February 26, 2025. Management will host a conference call and live audio webcast to discuss the results at 3:30 p.m. Central Time.

Hosting the call and webcast to review results for the fourth quarter 2024 will be Adam Sullivan, Chief Executive Officer, Denise Sterling, Chief Financial Officer, and Jon Charbonneau, Vice President, Investor Relations.

Conference Call and Webcast Event Summary

Date: February 26, 2025

Time: 3:30 PM CST (1:30 PM PST, 2:30 PM MST, 4:30 PM EST)

Participant Dial-In: 877-407-1875 / +1 215-268-9909

Investors with Internet access may listen to the live audio webcast directly by clicking here or via the Investors section of the Core Scientific, Inc. website, https://investors.corescientific.com, under "Events and Presentations." Please allow 10 minutes prior to the call to download and install any necessary audio software.

Audio Replay Options

An audio replay of the event will be archived on the Investor Relations section of the Company's website at https://investors.corescientific.com. The audio replay will also be available via telephone from Wednesday, February 26, 2025, at approximately 9:00 p.m. Central Time through Wednesday, March 12, 2025 at 11:59 p.m. Eastern Time. Dial (877) 660-6853 / (201) 612-7415 and use Access ID: 13751642.

ABOUT CORE SCIENTIFIC

Core Scientific, Inc. is a leader in digital infrastructure for digital assets mining and high-performance computing. We operate dedicated, purpose-built facilities for digital asset mining and are a premier provider of digital infrastructure to our third-party customers. We employ our own large fleet of computers ("miners") to earn digital assets for our own account, we provide hosting services for large bitcoin mining customers and we are in the process of allocating and converting a significant portion of our nine operational data centers in Alabama (1), Georgia (2), Kentucky (1), North Carolina (1), North Dakota (1) and Texas (3), and our facility in development in Oklahoma to support artificial intelligence-related workloads under a series of contracts that entail the modification of certain of our data centers to deliver hosting services for high-performance computing.

FORWARD LOOKING STATEMENTS AND EXPLANATORY NOTES

This press release contains "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of the "safe harbor" provisions of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995, including but not limited to, statements regarding projections, estimates and forecasts of revenue and other financial and performance metrics, projections of market opportunity and expectations, the Company's ability to scale and grow its businesses, implement its business strategy, source necessary electrical energy, the advantages and expected growth of the Company and the Company's ability to source and retain talent. You can identify forward-looking statements by the fact that they do not relate strictly to historical or current facts. These statements may include words such as "aim," "estimate," "plan," "project," "forecast," "goal," "intend," "will," "expect," "anticipate," "believe," "seek," "target" or other similar expressions that predict or indicate future events or trends or that are not statements of historical matters. All forward looking statements are subject to risks and uncertainties that may cause actual results to differ materially, including: our ability to earn digital assets profitably and to attract customers for our current and future expected hosting infrastructure; our ability to maintain our competitive position in our businesses; our ability to raise additional capital to continue our expansion efforts or other operations; our need for significant electric power and the limited availability of power resources; the potential failure in our critical systems, facilities or services we provide; the physical risks and regulatory changes relating to climate change; potential significant changes to the method of validating blockchain transactions; our vulnerability to physical security breaches, which could disrupt our operations; a potential slowdown in market and economic conditions, particularly those impacting artificial intelligence, high value computing, the blockchain industry and the blockchain hosting market; the identification of material weaknesses in our internal control over financial reporting; price volatility of digital assets and bitcoin in particular; the "halving" and other reduction of rewards available on the Bitcoin network, affecting our ability to generate revenue; the potential that insufficient awards from digital asset mining could disincentivize transaction processors from expending processing power on a particular network, which could negatively impact the utility of the network and further reduce the value of its digital assets; potential changes in the interpretive positions of the SEC or its staff with respect to digital asset mining firms; the increasing likelihood that U.S. federal and state legislatures and regulatory agencies will enact laws and regulations to regulate digital assets and digital asset intermediaries; increasing scrutiny and changing expectations with respect to our ESG policies; the effectiveness of our compliance and risk management methods; the adequacy of our sources of recovery if the digital assets held by us are lost, stolen or destroyed due to third-party digital asset services; and our level of indebtedness and our current liquidity constraints affecting our financial condition and ability to service our indebtedness. Any such forward-looking statements represent management's estimates and beliefs as of the date of this press release. While we may elect to update such forward-looking statements at some point in the future, we disclaim any obligation to do so, even if subsequent events cause our views to change.

data.title: Mexico Economic Activity Shrinks in December
data.description:
Economic activity in Mexico decreased 0.4% year-on-year in December 2024, the first contraction in six months, after a 0.5% rise in November, and compared to forecasts of a 0.3% rise.

The agricultural sector dropped 8%, construction plunged 7.5%, mining was down 6.3% and manufacturing decreased 0.6%.

On the other hand, the services sector went up 1.3%, namely professional, scientific and technical services (10.1%).

Financial and insurance services (6.7%) and retail trade (1.9%) were also higher.

Meanwhile, compared to the previous month, economic activity shrank 1%.
